verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is primarily mitochondrial inner-membrane resident but reaches the cell surface in documented contexts including tumor cells, endothelial cells, and neuronal cells, where it serves functionally distinct roles (viral entry facilitation, tumor-T cell recognition, extracellular ATP generation). This conditional surface expression via cell-type-dependent trafficking represents stable surface accessibility in specific biological contexts, not transient recruitment. Hepatocyte and endothelial cell surface activity confirmed by multiple publications.
